weka.associations.spade

Class Sequence

  • java.lang.Object
    • weka.associations.spade.Sequence
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Serializable

    public class Sequence
extends Object
implements Serializable
    Represents a sequence (ordered list of elements/itemsets) in the SPADE algorithm. Each sequence has an associated IdList for support counting. A sequence is represented as: <{a,b}, {c}, {d,e}> meaning item a and b occur together, then c, then d and e together.
    Author:
    Weka SPADE Implementation
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      Sequence()
      Constructor.
      Sequence(List<Element> elements)
      Constructor.
      Sequence(String item)
      Constructor.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void addElement(Element element)
      Adds an element to the end of this sequence.
      Sequence copy()
      Creates a copy of this sequence.
      boolean equals(Object obj) 
      Element getElement(int index)
      Returns the element at the given index.
      List<Element> getElements()
      Returns the list of elements in this sequence.
      IdList getIdList()
      Returns the ID-List associated with this sequence.
      Element getLastElement()
      Returns the last element of this sequence.
      String getLastItem()
      Returns the last item of the last element (used for atom identification when building equivalence classes).
      int getSupport()
      Returns the support of this sequence.
      int hashCode() 
      boolean isSequenceExtension()
      Determines if this atom was generated by a sequence extension (the last item is in its own element) vs an itemset extension (the last item was added to the last element of the prefix).
      int itemCount()
      Returns the total number of items across all elements.
      Sequence itemsetExtend(String item)
      Creates a new sequence by extending this sequence with an itemset extension (adding item to the last element).
      int length()
      Returns the number of elements in this sequence.
      Sequence sequenceExtend(String item)
      Creates a new sequence by extending this sequence with a sequence extension (adding item as a new element at the end).
      void setIdList(IdList idList)
      Sets the ID-List for this sequence.
      void setSupport(int support)
      Sets the support of this sequence.
      String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this sequence.
      String toStringWithSupport(int totalSequences)
      Returns a formatted string with support information.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        getClass, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • Sequence

        public Sequence()
        Constructor. Creates an empty sequence.

      • Sequence

        public Sequence(String item)
        Constructor. Creates a sequence with a single element containing one item.
        Parameters:
        item - the single item

      • Sequence

        public Sequence(List<Element> elements)
        Constructor. Creates a sequence from a list of elements.
        Parameters:
        elements - the list of elements

    • Method Detail

      • addElement

        public void addElement(Element element)
        Adds an element to the end of this sequence.
        Parameters:
        element - the element to add

      • getElements

        public List<Element> getElements()
        Returns the list of elements in this sequence.
        Returns:
        the elements

      • getElement

        public Element getElement(int index)
        Returns the element at the given index.
        Parameters:
        index - the index
        Returns:
        the element

      • length

        public int length()
        Returns the number of elements in this sequence.
        Returns:
        the length

      • itemCount

        public int itemCount()
        Returns the total number of items across all elements. This is the "k" in "k-sequence".
        Returns:
        the total item count

      • getIdList

        public IdList getIdList()
        Returns the ID-List associated with this sequence.
        Returns:
        the ID-List

      • setIdList

        public void setIdList(IdList idList)
        Sets the ID-List for this sequence.
        Parameters:
        idList - the ID-List to set

      • getSupport

        public int getSupport()
        Returns the support of this sequence.
        Returns:
        the support count

      • setSupport

        public void setSupport(int support)
        Sets the support of this sequence.
        Parameters:
        support - the support count

      • getLastElement

        public Element getLastElement()
        Returns the last element of this sequence.
        Returns:
        the last element, or null if empty

      • getLastItem

        public String getLastItem()
        Returns the last item of the last element (used for atom identification when building equivalence classes).
        Returns:
        the last item, or null if empty

      • isSequenceExtension

        public boolean isSequenceExtension()
        Determines if this atom was generated by a sequence extension (the last item is in its own element) vs an itemset extension (the last item was added to the last element of the prefix). For atom identification in equivalence classes: - Sequence extension: last element has 1 item - Itemset extension: last element has >1 items
        Returns:
        true if this is a sequence extension atom

      • copy

        public Sequence copy()
        Creates a copy of this sequence.
        Returns:
        a new Sequence with copied elements

      • sequenceExtend

        public Sequence sequenceExtend(String item)
        Creates a new sequence by extending this sequence with a sequence extension (adding item as a new element at the end).
        Parameters:
        item - the item to add as a new element
        Returns:
        the extended sequence

      • itemsetExtend

        public Sequence itemsetExtend(String item)
        Creates a new sequence by extending this sequence with an itemset extension (adding item to the last element).
        Parameters:
        item - the item to add to the last element
        Returns:
        the extended sequence

        public boolean equals(Object obj)
        Overrides:
        equals in class Object

        public int hashCode()
        Overrides:
        hashCode in class Object

      • toString

        public String toString()
        Returns a string representation of this sequence. Format: <{a,b}, {c}, {d}>
        Overrides:
        toString in class Object
        Returns:
        the string representation

      • toStringWithSupport

        public String toStringWithSupport(int totalSequences)
        Returns a formatted string with support information.
        Parameters:
        totalSequences - the total number of sequences for percentage
        Returns:
        formatted string
